surface_forms: California has done so much to protect things
description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Kentucky (US:  , UK: ), officially the Commonwealth of Kentucky, is a landlocked state in the Southeastern region of the United States.

description: university
description_wiki: Eastern Kentucky University (Eastern or EKU) is a public university in Richmond, Kentucky, United States.

description: public, historically black, land grant university in Frankfort, Kentucky, United States
description_wiki: Kentucky State University (KSU, and KYSU) is a public historically black land-grant university in Frankfort, Kentucky, United States.

description: seat of Harris County, and largest city in State of Texas, United States
description_wiki: Houston (  HEW-stən) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Texas and the Southern United States.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: Appalachian-American community organization
description_wiki: Kentuckians for the Commonwealth (KFTC) is a grassroots community organization under the original name of the Kentuckian Fair Tax Coalition.

description: researcher
44th presidential administration and cabinet of the USA (2009-2017)
description_wiki: Barack Obama's tenure as the 44th president of the United States began with his first inauguration on January 20, 2009, and ended on January 20, 2017.

description: metropolitan area in California, United States
description_wiki: The San Francisco Bay Area, commonly known as the Bay Area, is a region of California surrounding and including San Francisco Bay, and anchored by the cities of Oakland, San Francisco, and San Jose.

description: region in northern of U.S. state of California
description_wiki: Silicon Valley is a region in Northern California that is a global center for high technology and innovation.

description: city in Santa Clara County, California, United States
description_wiki: Los Altos ( ; Spanish for "The Heights") is a city in Santa Clara County, California, in the San Francisco Bay Area.

description: city in and county seat of Santa Cruz County, California, United States
description_wiki: Santa Cruz (Spanish for 'Holy Cross') is the largest city in and the county seat of Santa Cruz County, California.

description: annual event on April 22
description_wiki: Earth Day is an annual event on April 22 to demonstrate support for environmental protection.

description: American biologist and environmentalist (1932–2026)
description_wiki: Paul Ralph Ehrlich (May 29, 1932 – March 13, 2026) was an American biologist, author, and environmentalist known for his predictions and warnings about the consequences of population growth, including famine and resource depletion.

description: private research university in Stanford, California, USA
description_wiki: Leland Stanford Junior University, commonly referred to as Stanford University, is a private research university in Stanford, California, United States.

description: public research university in Corvallis, Oregon, United States
description_wiki: Oregon State University (OSU) is a public land-grant research university in Corvallis, Oregon, United States.

description: public university in Berkeley, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Berkeley (UC Berkeley, Berkeley, Cal, or California) is a public land-grant research university in the Southside and Northside neighborhoods of Berkeley, California, United States.

aliases: State Teachers College of San Francisco
description: university in San Francisco, California, USA
description_wiki: San Francisco State University (San Francisco State, SF State and SFSU) is a public research university in San Francisco, California, United States.

description: private research university in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Carnegie Mellon University (CMU) is a private research university in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, United States.

description: Swiss psychologist, biologist, logician, philosopher and academic (1896–1980)
description_wiki: Jean William Fritz Piaget (UK: , US: ; French: [ʒɑ̃ pjaʒɛ]; (9 August 1896 – 16 September 1980) was a Swiss psychologist known for his work on child development.
